title: From fragility to resilience through the lens of climate and mobility
content: This report outlines how climate change, conflict and fragility converge in the same regions, driving displacement and heightening disaster risk in countries least able to manage shocks.

title: Housing as climate resilience in Asia-Pacific cities
content: Asia‑Pacific cities face rising climate risks as nearly 700 million people live in hazard‑prone informal settlements. Affordable, resilient housing is essential for reducing urban vulnerability and accelerating progress toward SDG 11.

title: Record-shattering March temperatures in Western North America virtually impossible without climate change
content: A record‑breaking March heatwave across western North America was made virtually impossible without human‑driven climate change, with temperatures up to 17°C above average and severe risks to health, water supplies and ecosystems.

title: The influence of uncertainty: How a psychologist is improving disaster decision making
content: In this instalment in the Careers in Resilience series, PreventionWeb interviewed Susan, reflecting on her career and exploring what it takes to support better decision-making in the face of uncertainty.

title: Cooling cities, not just algorithms: Making AI work for India’s most vulnerable
content: Artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly shaping how cities plan and deliver services. The key question is not whether AI will drive India’s climate response, but whether it can do so equitably—protecting women, informal workers, and the urban poor.

title: First world map shows impact of the tidal pulse in coastal rivers
content: Researchers led by the Technical University of Munich (TUM) have created the first global atlas of the influence of tides on coastal rivers.

title: Facilitating successful debriefs
content: This online masterclass provides participants with the tools and resources needed to strengthen their ability to conduct effective debriefings.

title: Comparing the effectiveness of different financial mechanisms in women’s disaster resilience in the coastal city of Mahajanga, Madagascar
content: This research explores how different financial mechanisms support women to socioeconomically cope with disasters.

title: Higher Council for Civil Defense (HCfCD)
content: The Higher Council of Civil Defence (HCCD) is the main Palestinian decision-making body on disaster risk management.

title: Exploring climate resilience in a low-middle income country: The impact of socio-economic incentives on informal workers in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan
content: This study examines how socio-economic incentives, particularly one-time cash grants and the Benazir Income Support Program (BISP), contribute to the climate resilience of informal sector workers in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

title: National consultant – Development of localization model (Vientiane, Lao PDR)
content: The purpose of this consultancy is to facilitate the development and documentation of a country (i.e., LDPP)-specific Localization Model, capturing how localization has been defined, operationalized, and institutionalized through the partnership.

title: Chief of Section, P-5, New York Liaison Office
content: The position is responsible for the UNDRR New York Liaison Office and reports to the Chief of Intergovernmental Processes, Interagency Cooperation and Partnerships Branch, who is based in Geneva.

title: National Consultant – Development of Localization Model (Manila, Philippines)
content: ADPC is seeking a Manila-based national consultant to develop and document a localization model for disaster preparedness and climate resilience in the Philippines.

title: Reclaim Finance
content: Reclaim Finance is a non-governmental research and campaigning organization. It was founded in March 2020 by Lucie Pinson, winner of the 2020 Goldman Environmental Prize, with the aim of making finance work for the climate.

title: New model to forecast space weather on way
content: Aberystwyth scientists are developing a new model using coronagraph data to map the Sun’s magnetic field more accurately, improving forecasts of solar storms that threaten satellites, power grids and communications.

title: Barbados Implementation Modalities (BIM): A new chapter for loss and damage action - Technical partnership with PPI
content: BIM open the first funding window of the Loss and Damage Fund, offering USD 5–20 million per request. With half reserved for SIDS and LDCs, BIM supports country‑led action, and PPI helps nations design and deliver strong proposals.

title: Water injustice in a changing climate: A literature review of empirical cases across OECD river basins
content: This paper examines how climate change is reshaping water systems in ways that can create or reinforce water injustice, particularly across OECD river basins.

title: Exercising saves lives: Designing and implementing effective disaster response training
content: Structured disaster‑response training from workshops to full‑scale exercises builds coordination, tests plans and strengthens readiness. Continuous, realistic practice helps authorities reduce disaster impacts and protect lives.

title: Unlocking nature for resilience: effective policies for enabling nature-based solutions
content: This event convenes policymakers, development partners, and practitioners for a strategic discussion on policies to enable NBS upscaling for climate resilience and risk reduction.

title: Research provides timely views of warming’s impact on Alaska glaciers
content: Alaska’s glaciers respond to climate change by melting for three additional weeks with every 1 degree Celsius increase in the average summer temperature, data from satellite-mounted radars show.

title: Innovative approaches to forest fire management and agricultural transformation in northern Thailand: Developing fire risk mapping and management systems – integrating disruptive technologies for impact monitoring
content: This report presents the findings from the first of two activities conducted with the primary aim of gaining insights into the increasing threats posed by wildfire outbreaks to both forests and local communities in Thailand.

title: A study examines the Spanish experience with climate shelters as an example for the world
content: A scientific team has authored a comment article in the Nature Climate Change journal on how to use climate shelters to protect the population from extreme heat, with a particular focus on Spain.

title: Indigenous Australians always come off worst in disasters. This needs to stop
content: Indigenous communities are often the worst hit when major disasters strike. The support provided to Indigenous communities appears to be different to that available to non-Indigenous residents.

title: Disaster displacement risk in Guatemala: An overview of the risk of future displacement
content: This report is designed to help governments and their partners to reduce risk, strengthen resilience and minimise the impacts of disasters, including internal displacement.

title: National Caucus of Environmental Legislators
content: Created by and for state legislators, the Caucus serves as a resource on environmental issues through legislative research, organized events, and by facilitating collaboration between lawmakers working on similar issues.

title: Farmers in northern Iraq turn to new ways to cultivate their land
content: To help farmers adapt, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) is supporting training in dry farming techniques, zero tillage, and conservation agriculture – practical approaches designed for areas where rainfall is increasingly limited.

title: Urban heat: Assessing risks and identifying interventions
content: This Technical Note aims to help readers plan a heat risk assessment and identify policies and investments to address the identified risks. Section 1 presents key considerations for planning a heat risk assessment.

title: Is extreme heat fueling teen depression?
content: Research has long suggested that temperature can affect mental health. That’s because extreme temperatures can stress the body, triggering biological changes such as systemic inflammation. Can this cause depressive symptoms, too?

title: Urban heat: Planning and implementing investments for heat resilience
content: This document's purpose is not to further assess or quantify heat risks, but to support cities in translating existing evidence into concrete, investable action and project concepts, and where the pathway from assessment to implementation remains unclear.

title: Paper outlines pathways to equitable flood adaptation
content: Many flood adaptation measures exacerbate existing environmental injustices. A Cary-coauthored perspective suggests strategies to break the cycle.

title: Geospatial innovations addressing critical water data gaps in Asia
content: Satellite data and AI are helping Asian countries close critical water‑data gaps, improving flood and drought planning, strengthening lake‑ecosystem monitoring, and supporting smarter, climate‑resilient water governance across the region.

title: Global Tsunami Model Association (GTM)
content: The Global Tsunami Model (GTM) Network aims to save lives, reduce losses, and enhance resilience by advancing tsunami science, providing expert information, and promoting dialogue about tsunami hazards and risks.

title: Future Proof
content: Future Proof was born from the Paulele Hale Association (TPHA), a nonprofit founded to pioneer climate-resilient, affordable housing.

title: Emergencia Animal
content: Emergencia Animal's mission is to integrate animal welfare into disaster risk reduction frameworks across Spain, ensuring that families, animal shelters, and communities can effectively prepare for, respond to, and recover from emergencies.

title: Strengthening urban climate resilience: Socio-economic and institutional drivers among refugee populations in Lilongwe, Malawi
content: Urban refugees face compounded climate vulnerabilities that remain poorly understood.

title: World Intangible Cultural Heritage Federation (WICHF)
content: World Intangible Cultural Heritage Federation (WICHF) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to documenting, safeguarding and promoting intangible cultural heritage, traditional knowledge and community-based cultural practices.

title: Bahar Organization
content: Bahar is a local organization delivering support across northern Syria and in Iraq.

title: Assessing village and savings associations (VSLA’s) contribution to urban resilience: Evidence from Northern Ghana
content: This study explores the role of Village Savings and Loan Associations (VSLAs) in enhancing urban resilience to climate change in Northern Ghana, focusing on the cities of Tamale and Bolgatanga.

title: The United Nations World Water Development Report 2026: Water for all people: equal rights and opportunities
content: The United Nations World Water Development Report 2026: Water for All People – Equal Rights and Opportunities highlights how unequal access to water and sanitation services impacts health, education, livelihoods and safety.

title: What disaster preparedness experts have packed in their go bags
content: Climate change is making extreme weather events and natural hazards more frequent and intense. Here’s how to prepare before they hit.

title: Evaluating the multiplier effect of micro-insurance on climate resilience among motorcycle taxi drivers in Dar es Salaam
content: This study investigates the multiplier effect of micro-insurance on climate resilience and socio-economic stability among motorcycle taxi (boda-boda) drivers in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania.

title: Florida farmers face rising climate risks from extreme weather
content: In the last decade, extreme weather has become a greater economic liability for Florida farmers. In response, farmers are adopting practices designed to reduce long-term costs and improve resilience.

title: Socio-economic pathways to urban climate resilience: Exploring governance and incentives for equitable climate action in global south cities
content: This study investigates the socio-economic pathways to urban climate resilience in the cities of Mbabane and Manzini, Eswatini, focusing on the role of governance and socio-economic incentives in fostering equitable climate action.

title: The impact of climate risks on European sovereign credit ratings and long-term borrowing costs
content: This paper presents an empirical assessment of how climate‑related risks shape sovereign credit ratings and long‑term borrowing costs across the EU‑27 and the United Kingdom.

title: International Review of Economics & Finance
content: IREF publishes high-quality theoretical and empirical research across the broad fields of economics and finance.
